title: Review an eval for leakage and significance
description: Read an evaluation setup and report whether the golden set is leak-free, the metric fits the goal, and wins are significant.
body: Use when asked whether an eval result can be trusted. Actions: inspect how the golden set was sourced and whether it overlaps training/prompt material; check the metric against the stated goal for gameability; verify reported differences come with variance and a significance check; confirm the dataset version is stamped on the score. Do not accept a headline score without checking its dataset and statistics. Report and record the exact weaknesses, classifying each as leakage, gameable-metric, no-significance, or unversioned-data. This skill is read-only and must not modify the dataset without runtime permission. Validation signals include a confirmed cross-boundary overlap or a missing confidence interval. Risk boundary: a leaked or non-significant result presented as a win is a blocking finding; require re-evaluation.
